figures.caption: Figure 2: CSG turns rules into governed trajectories, and constraint native learning improves utility only within the admissible set.

Table 2: Coverage of mechanism families for LLM-agent safety. Existing mechanisms provide useful local controls, but they do not by themselves maintain constraints as inherited, checkable, and auditable state across full multi-agent trajectories.

Figure 1: (a) Multi-agent execution shifts safety from final-answer checking to trajectory-level constraint preservation; (b) constraint drift identifies where constraints lose operational force across memory, authority, information flow, accountability, and optimization.
Table 1: Diagnostic drift modes derived from recurring interfaces in LLM-based multi-agent trajectories.
